74065.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: 'The low is almost in'
74065.body:
Polymarket's UMA vote upholds 'No' outcome on Strategy bitcoin sale market despite backlash | Bitmine to launch STRC-style preferred stock offering amid $9.2 billion unrealized ETH losses | Coinbase launches pre-IPO perpetual futures, starting with SpaceX | Arthur Hayes sells entire HYPE and NEAR positions, citing AI IPOs and US midterms |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Thursday! *Bitcoin's* 14% slide over the past week has prompted analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403659/the-rally-that-wasnt-bitcoin-slides-14-in-one-week-as-etf-outflows-strategy-sale-and-oil-prices-hit-sentiment?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) to dub its prior move "the rally that wasn't," as a rare *Strategy* sale, a $4.4 billion spot ETF outflow streak, and a tougher macro backdrop combined to sour sentiment and push the cryptocurrency to its lowest level in four months.

In today's newsletter, *Standard Chartered* says the *bitcoin* bottom is near, *Polymarket's UMA* vote upholds a "No" outcome on *Strategy's bitcoin* sale market, *Bitmine* is set to launch an *STRC* -style preferred stock offering, and more.

Meanwhile, *JPMorgan* said ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403676/jpmorgan-crypto-bill-narrow-window-passage-this-year?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) the crypto market structure bill may have only a narrow window for passage this year.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯУЙ 'The low is almost in': Standard Chartered says bitcoin bottom near after tough week for crypto*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/403625/the-low-is-almost-in-standard-chartered-says-bitcoin-bottom-near-after-tough-week-for-crypto?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick* said a *bitcoin* bottom is "almost in," arguing that resilient spot ETF holdings and a likely *Strategy* buyback suggest the worst of the sell-off may be over.

* Kendrick now views current levels as a "buying zone," despite bitcoin falling more than 22% over the past month and trading around $63,000.
* The analyst said a key reason for his change in outlook since a $50,000 bitcoin call back in February is that spot bitcoin ETF holdings

74061.subject: American capitalism has taken an apocalyptic turn
74061.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGp1PfXQiZIH_frGHdYfYtyQZ3NQemoU1TdfPjlO8Mr-pISvRBMBc83WmM5eBR9N7q7xWaM5SjBv11B0YPaUCZyaJbA_yYjIpUJIyvz 

June 4th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGqNW8zUdTYerUo2PMYWbBEzkughTut1hzJ1LUAm6gkMUMN_4PKtAHHp8XQvKzRWdjM_37gfSCAWey3gCouLUzPZfV94rcS19WbP9M7 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGrzm6tQSR8cg_xvOT9WV5WYnqn_f7TemE38wrpxlBku569qoj-Y6E1b0lLwfR9cjMl2LzdLrQ5dZNjklOKVa3p8YikOBzL6PNVe5Cg 



 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 “America is an experiment in terrifying yourself to riches,” reads an intriguing sentence in this week’s Schumpeter column. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGs6Squ8iplqwWaEvTh5PVI1dd6sV9ao_AsRhUJBUBCwbhqHZJfPu_H8liMk455aKLoyhY2OxtwqDbklrptg5V3U5i7A9Xanys 
Our columnist 
 notices that a lot of what’s driving the investment frenzy in sectors like artificial intelligence and defence is an apocalyptic view of the way the world is heading. Will investors profit from cataclysm or be overwhelmed by it?


 A murder in Southampton last year has become a political controversy that compounds the tragedy. There are questions to answer about how the police responded to the stabbing of a y

рЯІ† AIвАЩs Crypto News Brief: Extreme fear dominates as Bitcoin crashes to a four-month low, liquidating $1.6 billion in longs. Record ETF outflows and a symbolic MicroStrategy sale have shifted sentiment toward a structural de-risking phase. рЯЪ®Bitcoin Crashes to $61,000 Amidst Massive Liquidation Wave Bitcoin plummeted to a four-month low of $61,300 today, triggering over $1.61 billion in market-wide liquidations. The sharp decline was fueled by record institutional outflows and heightened geopolitical tensions, wiping out over 290,000 leveraged traders. Read full story рЯУЙSpot Bitcoin ETFs Record Historic 12-Day Outflow Streak U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have shed a cumulative $3.58 billion over the last 12 trading days, the longest redemption streak since their 2024 launch. BlackRockвАЩs IBIT saw its second-largest single-day withdrawal, signaling a major cooling of institutional appetite. See details рЯТ∞MicroStrategy Breaks "Never Sell" Stance with Symbolic Disposal MicroStrategy disclosed the sale of 32 Bitcoin to fund preferred stock distributions, its first disposal since 2022. While operationally immaterial, the move has rattled investor confidence in the firmвАЩs long-standing permanent treasury strategy. Read more рЯПЫпЄПRobinhood Enters Canada via $180M WonderFi Acquisition Robinhood Markets has officially closed its acquisition of Canadian digital asset firm WonderFi, bringing 300,000 new customers into its ecosystem. The deal provides Robinhood with immediate regulatory standing and infrastructure to scale its crypto services across North America. See full announcement рЯУЙEthereum Slumps to 14-Month Low as ETF Optimism Fades Ethereum (ETH) dropped below the critical $1,900 level to touch a 14-month low of $1,738 during today's market rout. Analysts cite a "rally that wasn't," as delays in ETF filings and broader macro headwinds weigh heavily on the second-largest asset. Read full story рЯФДInstitutional Rotation: Capital Shifts from Crypto to AI Equities Market data reveals a significant rotation of capital out of crypto risk assets and into AI-focused tech stocks. This strategic shift is being driven by sticky inflation data and a narrowing premium on crypto-treasury corporate strategies. See market analysis рЯУИRipple (XRP) Institutional Inflows Defy Broader Market Bleed Despite the global market crash, XRP-based investment product

74053.subject: Andrew Yang Buys Helium Mobile
74053.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Gm, and happy Thursday!¬†

Crypto slipped lower as equities closed WednesdayвАЩs session in the red and futures traded lower overnight. ETFs continue to show outflows while STRC trades off par, adding pressure to the persistent bear.¬†

Meanwhile, Andrew YangвАЩs Noble Mobile acquires Helium Mobile. What does Noble see that others might have missed?

вАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФвАФ

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

BTC traded back down to $61.3K following five straight days of negative price action. This marks the lowest price in 100 days as the rally of Q2 has largely retraced and proved to be counter-trend. While BTC is knocking on the lows, equities remain ~1.3% off of their all-time highs. Notably, within crypto, BTC is leading to the downside. Over the past week, the majority of crypto indices have outperformed BTC, raising the question of whether breadth and dispersion shows internal health, or if correlations move back towards 1 for the next move lower in alts. Perps remain the top performing sector on the week, as LIT, HYPE, and MYX trade strong.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/15ad09b0-ac5f-4943-83df-56ebe0f8fa9b/image.png?t=1780579878)
Caption: 

ETF flows havenвАЩt found their floor, either. For BTC and ETH, daily net flows remained stubbornly negative throughout the second half of May, with the selling continuing into June. Meanwhile, SOL and HYPE ETFs show modest inflows.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1c52425c-2ad2-4cfd-a669-dd0530763693/image.png?t=1780579878)
Caption: 

With persistent negative returns on BTC, Saylor is under pressure as STRC closed Wednesday at $95, $5 off par. StrategyвАЩs moves over the past week, using cash to buy back convertible notes and selling 32 BTC, may have led to some unintended consequences. To begin, the viability of STRC embeds the assumption that the BTC purchased with proceeds from STRC issuance will appreciate in excess of the dividend obligation, net of price impact, which has certainly not been the case since issuance began amid BTCвАЩs bear market. Similarly, a reduced cash pile from the convertible repurchase lowers StrategyвАЩs cash runway for dividend payments, raising the concern that MSTR would need to sell more BTC to cover this. The market smells this out, with BTC moving risk-off in advance of these flows. Effectively, STRC trading off par is like credit spreads widening, with
